steps.title: Spot the defining claim being tested
steps.content: The researchers claim the sounds are “directed primarily at predators”—a defensive function. To support this, you need evidence that predators actually respond to the sounds.

steps.title: Find evidence of predator response
steps.content:
Choice B demonstrates that when predators (chickens and yellow warblers) hear caterpillar sounds, they stop attacking—directly confirming the sounds serve a protective function against predators.

Choices A, C, and D address species variation, frequency detection, or sound consistency, none of which shows that predator behavior changes in response to the sounds.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
